Title: The phenol-2-monooxygenase FgPhm1 regulates DON synthesis, pathogenicity and environmental stress response in Fusarium graminearum

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenol/catechol metabolism; indirect regulation of trichothecene (DON) biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenol-to-catechol conversion; secondary metabolite regulation linked to trichothecene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream phenol oxidation step; indirect upstream controller of DON accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Responsible for conversion of phenol to catechol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly promotes target product by enabling expression of DON biosynthetic genes and toxisome formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for DON accumulation not established as catalytic rate-limiting step for phenol metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor in Fusarium head blight pathogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal toxisomes and culture medium under TBI conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during conidiation; induced by low phenol concentration; significantly upregulated by H2O2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during conidiation; induced by low phenol concentration; significantly upregulated by H2O2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TBI medium; phenol; catechol; H2O2; SDS; CFW; Congo red; NaCl; KCl; nitrogen starvation; rapamycin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DON production; deletion sharply reduces DON, disrupts toxisome formation, and causes tolerance to phenol but sensitivity to catechol and oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FgPHM1 (FGSG_00489) caused a major reduction in DON production in TBI medium. 2. FgPHM1 deletion reduced expression of TRI4, TRI6, TRI10 and TRI12 and largely abolished visible FgTri1/4-GFP toxisomes. 3. FgPHM1 encodes a phenol-2-monooxygenase implicated in phenol-to-catechol metabolism, with the mutant showing phenol tolerance but catechol sensitivity. 4. FgPHM1 is also required for normal growth, reproduction, ROS production, stress tolerance, autophagy, and pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation with native-promoter FgPhm1-GFP; DON quantification in TBI medium; RT-qPCR of TRI genes; toxisome imaging with FgTri1-GFP/FgTri4-GFP; western blot of GFP fusions; stress assays; RNA-seq; ROS staining
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing DON contamination and Fusarium head blight virulence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes severe defects in growth, conidiation, sexual reproduction, stress responses, ROS production and autophagy, indicating broad pleiotropic effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among six phenol-2-monooxygenase homologs identified in the genome, FgPHM1 was the only one whose deletion caused strong phenotypes relevant to metabolism and virulence; RNA-seq DEGs were enriched in secondary metabolite biosynthesis, carbon metabolism and pyruvate metabolism

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Metabolic regulator/common oxidoreductase affecting transcription of secondary metabolite genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of TRI gene expression and toxisome formation; positive regulation of ROS-associated and stress-response processes; regulation of autophagy under nitrogen starvation or rapamycin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary metabolite biosynthesis, especially DON biosynthesis; phenol/catechol metabolism; ROS homeostasis; autophagy-related nutrient response
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FgTRI4; FgTRI6; FgTRI10; FgTRI12; toxisome formation machinery/markers FgTri1 and FgTri4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or maintains expression of FgTRI4, FgTRI6, FgTRI10 and FgTRI12; promotes accumulation/visibility of FgTri1 and FgTri4 in toxisomes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Phenol at low concentration; H2O2; nitrogen starvation; rapamycin
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No stable regulatory complex demonstrated; colocalization shown with nucleoplasmic marker FgNcbp2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of DON accumulation because deletion strongly reduced DON production and impaired both TRI gene expression and toxisome formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Toxisomes and culture medium under toxin-inducing conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during conidiation; upregulated by low phenol and by H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during conidiation; upregulated by low phenol and by H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TBI medium; phenol; catechol; H2O2; nitrogen starvation; rapamycin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion sharply decreases DON biosynthesis by lowering TRI gene expression and blocking toxisome formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FgPHM1 is the only phenol-2-monooxygenase homolog with strong developmental and metabolic phenotypes in this study. 2. FgPHM1 positively regulates DON biosynthesis through effects on TRI gene transcription and toxisome formation. 3. FgPHM1 also regulates ROS production, stress adaptation and autophagy-related responses.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion/complementation; RT-qPCR; confocal localization and toxisome imaging; western blot; RNA-seq; metabolite assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising fungal control target to suppress DON contamination and pathogenicity in cereals
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Targeting FgPHM1 may alter multiple fungal physiological processes beyond toxin production
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a transcription factor, the paper demonstrates a regulatory role over TRI gene expression and secondary metabolism, so it is included here as a regulatory core gene
